text: Brainstormed additional worked examples pitched at the living-workspace sweet spot (modest, no heavy database), then settled on building a new "life-tracker" example seeded from a BotBeam dashboard tab. Rather than screen-scrape, pulled the target tab's content via the BotBeam API and saved it verbatim as examples/life-tracker/seed/life-infrastructure-backlog.md, stamped with source and time. Scaffolded examples/life-tracker/ by copying template/ (repo.py, server.py, validate.py, dashboard capability, index template) with data/ and schemas/ intentionally still empty, and drafted examples/life-tracker/OVERVIEW.md proposing a three-kind model: area (the seven life areas from the backlog), project (one outcome in one area), and action (one concrete step with a dated progress log), plus rules like flagging stalled projects and treating the BotBeam tab as a write-only re-beamed view whose original stays in seed/. Registered life-tracker as a fourth example in the repo-root CLAUDE.md and recorded two deliberately-unresolved design questions (the time/staleness model and an uncertain prioritization capability) as open questions in OVERVIEW.md plus a memory note. Ended awaiting the user's sign-off on the overview before creating any schemas or migrating the seed into data/.
